reasoning_content: We need to determine if 439 is prime. Check divisibility: by 2? No, odd. By 3? Sum digits 4+3+9=16, not divisible by 3. By 5? Last digit not 0 or 5. By 7? 7*62=434, remainder 5, not divisible. 7*63=441, too high. By 11? Alternating sum: 4-3+9=10, not multiple of 11. By 13? 13*33=429, remainder 10; 13*34=442, too high. By 17? 17*25=425, remainder 14; 17*26=442, too high. By 19? 19*23=437, remainder 2; 19*24=456, too high. By 23? 23*19=437, remainder 2; 23*20=460. By 29? 29*15=435, remainder 4; 29*16=464. By 31? 31*14=434, remainder 5; 31*15=465. sqrt(439) ≈ 20.95, so we only need to check primes up to 19 (since 23^2=529 > 439). Actually up to 19 is enough because 19^2=361, 23^2=529, so check primes up to 19: 2,3,5,7,11,13,17,19. None divide. So it's prime. Answer yes.
